Character Breakdown: 面 (face) | 包 -> 勹 (archaic variant of 包[bao1]) + 巳 (6th earthly branch: 9-11 a.m., 4th solar month (5th May-5th June), year of the Snake)

Character Breakdown: 搬 -> 扌 + 舟 (boat) + 殳 (weapon)

grammar.explanation: Used to indicate two qualities or actions existing at the same time ('both... and...').

examples.chinese: 这家超市的蛋糕又好吃又便宜。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiā chāoshì de dàngāo yòu hǎochī yòu piányi.
examples.english: The cakes in this supermarket are both delicious and cheap.

grammar.explanation: Both mean 'or', but 还是 is used in questions, while 或者 is used in statements.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ hē chá háishì hē kāfēi?
examples.english: Do you drink tea or coffee?

examples.chinese: 我喝茶或者喝咖啡都可以。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ hē chá huòzhě hē kāfēi dōu kěyǐ.
examples.english: I can drink either tea or coffee.

grammar.point: The complement of direction: 来 (lái) and 去 (qù)
grammar.explanation: 来 indicates movement towards the speaker, while 去 indicates movement away from the speaker.

examples.pinyin: Tā shénme shíhou huílái?
examples.english: When will he come back?

grammar.point: The particle 着 (zhe) indicating the manner of an action
grammar.explanation: V1 + 着 + V2 indicates that V1 is the manner or state in which V2 is performed.

examples.chinese: 她总是笑着跟客人说话。
examples.pinyin: Tā zǒngshì xiào zhe gēn kèrén shuōhuà.
examples.english: She always speaks to customers with a smile.

title: 她总是笑着跟客人说话

grammar.explanation: 又 usually refers to an action that has already repeated, while 再 refers to an action that will repeat in the future.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ zěnme yòu chídào le?
examples.english: How come you are late again?

examples.pinyin: Qǐng zài shuō yí biàn.
examples.english: Please say it again.

grammar.explanation: Indicates that a quality or state changes over time ('more and more...').

examples.pinyin: Tiānqì yuè lái yuè rè le.
examples.english: The weather is getting hotter and hotter.

grammar.point: The potential complement 'V + 得/不 + Result/Direction'
grammar.explanation: Indicates whether a result or direction can be achieved.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ kàn qīngchu le.
examples.english: I saw it clearly.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ kàn bù qīngchu.
examples.english: I can't see it clearly.

grammar.point: Duration of an action: 'Verb + 了 + Time + 了'
grammar.explanation: Indicates that an action has been going on for a certain period and is still continuing.

examples.pinyin: Wǒmen rènshi wǔ nián le.
examples.english: We have known each other for five years (and still do).

grammar.explanation: Used to connect two identical interrogative pronouns to indicate that the second depends on the first.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ qù nǎr, wǒ jiù qù nǎr.
examples.english: Wherever you go, I will follow.

title: 她的汉语说得跟中国人一样好

grammar.explanation: Used to indicate that two things are the same.

examples.chinese: 他的汉语说得跟中国人一样好。
examples.pinyin: Tā de Hànyǔ shuō de gēn Zhōngguórén yíyàng hǎo.
examples.english: He speaks Chinese as well as a Chinese person.

Character Breakdown: 声 -> 士 (surname Shi) + 丨 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 2)) + No glyph available + 丨 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 2)) | 音 (sound)
Character Breakdown: 发 -> ㇗ + 丨 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 2)) + 丶 (see 點|点[dian3]) + 又 ((once) again) | 现 -> 王 (surname Wang) + 见 (to see)
Character Breakdown: 提 -> 扌 + 日 (sun) + 一 (one) + 龰 | 高 (high)

grammar.explanation: Used after a verb to indicate that someone recognizes something or something becomes manifest.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ méi kàn chūlái ma?
examples.english: Didn't you recognize it/him/her?

vocabulary.hint: Character Breakdown: 被 -> 衤 (clothes) + 皮 (skin)
Character Breakdown: 影 -> 日 (sun) + 亠 + 口 (mouth) + 小 (small) + 彡 | 响 -> 口 (mouth) + 宀 (roof) + 口 (mouth)
Character Breakdown: 发 -> ㇗ + 丨 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 2)) + 丶 (see 點|点[dian3]) + 又 ((once) again) | 现 -> 王 (surname Wang) + 见 (to see)
Character Breakdown: 注 -> 氵 (water) + 王 (surname Wang) + 丶 (see 點|点[dian3]) | 意 -> 音 (sound) + 心 (heart)

Character Breakdown: 解 -> 角 (horn) + 刀 (surname Dao) + 牛 (cow) | 决 -> 冫 ("ice" radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 15), occurring in 冰, 决, 次) + No glyph available + 丨 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 2)) + 人 (person)
Character Breakdown: 水 (water) | 平 -> 干 (surname Gan) + 丷 ("eight" component in Chinese characters)
Character Breakdown: 完 -> 宀 (roof) + 二 (two) + 儿 (son) | 成 -> 一 (one) + 丿 (radical in Chinese characters (Kangxi radical 4)) + ㇆ + 戈 (surname Ge)

grammar.explanation: Revisiting the passive voice where the subject is the receiver of the action.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ bèi tā yǐngxiǎng le.
examples.english: I was influenced by him.
